Title: Calcium-dependent Protein Kinase 5 (CPK5) positively modulates drought tolerance through phosphorylating ABA-Responsive Element Binding Factors in oilseed rape (Brassica napus L.)

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: BnaCPK5 is closely related phylogenetically to Arabidopsis CPK5 and rice CPK6 and belongs to Group I of the CPK family. Its localization at both the plasma membrane and nucleus suggests roles in sensing Ca2+ signals and directly modulating transcription factors. Interactions with BnaAHG3 and BnaHAB1 position BnaCPK5 within an ABA core signaling module analogous to the PYR/PYL–PP2C–SnRK2–ABF cascade described in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. BnaCPK5 encodes a Group I calcium-dependent protein kinase with an N-terminal kinase domain and C-terminal CaM-like domain containing four EF-hand motifs, and is predicted to be myristoylated and palmitoylated at its N-terminus.
2. BnaCPK5 localizes to both the plasma membrane and nucleus in plant cells, with N-terminal lipidation contributing to membrane association, and is expressed in roots, stems and leaves with higher transcript abundance in leaves.
3. Constitutive overexpression of BnaCPK5 in oilseed rape significantly enhances drought tolerance, as shown by higher survival rate, relative water content, proline accumulation, and reduced MDA under water deficit compared to GFP controls.
4. BnaCPK5 physically interacts with ABA-responsive transcription factors BnaABF3 and BnaABF4 in nuclei and phosphorylates them in vitro, increasing their transcriptional activity toward the RD29B promoter and stabilizing BnaABF3/4 proteins by reducing their 26S proteasome-dependent degradation.
5. BnaCPK5 also interacts with clade A PP2Cs BnaAHG3 and BnaHAB1, suggesting that its kinase activity is integrated into the canonical ABA signaling module of kinases and phosphatases that fine-tune ABA and drought responses.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of BnaCPK5 in oilseed rape enhances drought tolerance, associated with improved water status, increased proline accumulation, reduced lipid peroxidation, and modulation of ABA-responsive gene regulation via phosphorylation of BnaABF3 and BnaABF4.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Ca2+-dependent Ser/Thr protein kinase that autophosphorylates and phosphorylates BnaABF3 and BnaABF4, enhancing their transcriptional activity on the RD29B promoter and increasing their protein stability by reducing 26S proteasome-dependent degradation; physically interacts with clade A PP2Cs BnaAHG3 and BnaHAB1, which are expected to antagonize its kinase activity as negative regulators of ABA signaling.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA-dependent drought stress signaling pathway, where BnaCPK5 acts as a positive regulator that decodes Ca2+ signals and modulates core ABA-signaling transcription factors BnaABF3/4 and potentially interacts with clade A PP2Cs.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: BnaCPK5 --(Ca2+-dependent protein phosphorylation, activation)--> BnaABF3; BnaCPK5 --(Ca2+-dependent protein phosphorylation, activation)--> BnaABF4; BnaCPK5 phosphorylation of BnaABF3/4 -->(activation)--> RD29B promoter activity and LEA-like RD29B expression; BnaAHG3 --(protein–protein interaction, likely inhibition of kinase activity)--> BnaCPK5; BnaHAB1 --(protein–protein interaction, likely inhibition of kinase activity)--> BnaCPK5.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Calcium-dependent protein kinase (CPK) family; N-terminal Ser/Thr kinase domain, junction region, and C-terminal calmodulin-like domain with four EF-hand motifs; predicted N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation sites.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane and nucleus, based on BnaCPK5-GFP and BnaCPK5(G2A, C5S)-GFP localization in N. benthamiana epidermal cells with CBL1n-mCherry and NLS-mCherry markers.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BnaABF3 and BnaABF4 (ABA-responsive bZIP transcription factors), strongly interacting with BnaCPK5 in nuclei by BiFC and confirmed by Co-IP; additional interactions with clade A PP2Cs BnaAHG3 and BnaHAB1 detected by yeast two-hybrid and BiFC.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Constitutively expressed in roots, stems and leaves of one-month-old plants, with higher transcript levels in leaves than in stems or roots.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress imposed by withholding water from potted plants for 12 d followed by rewatering; osmotic stress during assays; treatment of N. benthamiana leaves with the 26S proteasome inhibitor MG132 (50 μM) in protein stability assays; mannitol-induced plasmolysis (500 mM) for subcellular localization.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: ABA-dependent drought tolerance and calcium-dependent protein kinase signaling in Brassica napus.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: After 12 d of water withholding and 7 d of rewatering, BnaCPK5-OE plants showed approximately 73% survival compared with about 12% in GFP-OE controls; BnaCPK5-OE plants maintained significantly higher leaf relative water content, accumulated more proline, and had significantly lower MDA levels than GFP-OE plants after 5–7 d of drought treatment (statistical significance P < 0.05 or P < 0.01).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under normal, well-watered conditions, BnaCPK5-overexpressing plants show no obvious differences in growth and development compared with GFP-overexpressing controls.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oilseed rape (Brassica napus) line DH12075 and derived 35S::BnaCPK5-3xFLAG overexpression lines; 35S::GFP-3xFLAG control lines; Nicotiana benthamiana plants for subcellular localization, BiFC, Co-IP, dual-luciferase and protein stability assays; E. coli BL21(Codon Plus) for recombinant protein expression; yeast strain Y2HGold for Y2H assays; Agrobacterium rhizogenes K599 for hairy root transformation; Agrobacterium tumefaciens GV3101 (pMP90) and GV3101 (pSoup) for transient expression.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RT-PCR cloning and sequence analysis; domain prediction and phylogenetic analysis; confocal microscopy of BnaCPK5-GFP and lipidation mutants in N. benthamiana; RT-qPCR of organ-specific expression; Agrobacterium-mediated stable transformation to generate 35S::BnaCPK5-3xFLAG overexpression lines; drought treatment by withholding water, followed by survival scoring; measurements of relative water content, proline, and MDA; yeast two-hybrid screening with BnaPP2Cs; BiFC in N. benthamiana; Co-immunoprecipitation; in vitro kinase assays using recombinant BnaCPK5 and BnaABF3/4 with ADP-Glo detection; dual-luciferase reporter assays with ProRD29B::LUC; modified dual-luciferase protein stability assays with MG132 treatment; statistical analysis with t-test and ANOVA.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate CPK gene previously identified from EST mining; full-length cDNA of BnaCPK5 was amplified from oilseed rape by RT-PCR using specific primers and cloned for functional studies.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: BnaCPK5 is a validated positive regulator of drought tolerance in oilseed rape, improving survival, water retention, osmoprotection, and reducing oxidative damage under water deficit. Its ability to phosphorylate and stabilize core ABA-signaling transcription factors (BnaABF3/4) and to integrate Ca2+ and ABA signaling makes it a promising target for genetic engineering or marker-assisted improvement of drought-resilient Brassica napus cultivars.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The RD29B reporter used in dual-luciferase assays is derived from Arabidopsis (AtRD29B), but the experimental results support a conserved role of BnaABF3 in activating ABRE-containing drought-responsive promoters. The balance between phosphorylation-mediated activation/stabilization (by kinases such as BnaCPK5) and ubiquitin–proteasome-mediated degradation is highlighted as a key regulatory mechanism for ABF/AREB transcription factors.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. BnaABF3 is an ABA-responsive bZIP transcription factor (ABF/AREB) from oilseed rape that interacts strongly with BnaCPK5 in the nucleus, as shown by BiFC and Co-IP assays.
2. BnaCPK5 directly phosphorylates BnaABF3 in vitro, as shown by fluorometric kinase assays using recombinant proteins.
3. In a dual-luciferase reporter system with the RD29B promoter, co-expression of BnaABF3 with BnaCPK5 significantly enhances RD29B-driven LUC activity compared with BnaABF3 alone, indicating that BnaCPK5-mediated phosphorylation increases BnaABF3 transcriptional activity.
4. In modified dual-luciferase assays, co-expression of BnaCPK5 increases the protein level and LUC activity of BnaABF3-LUC fusion proteins, and this stabilization effect disappears upon MG132 treatment, indicating that BnaABF3 is subject to 26S proteasome-dependent degradation and that phosphorylation by BnaCPK5 enhances its stability.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BnaABF3 functions as a nuclear ABA-responsive transcription factor whose phosphorylation by BnaCPK5 enhances its transcriptional activation of the RD29B promoter and increases its protein stability, supporting a positive role in ABA-dependent drought-responsive gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: As a bZIP ABF/AREB transcription factor, BnaABF3 binds ABREs and activates transcription of RD29B; phosphorylation by BnaCPK5 enhances its transcriptional activity and increases its protein stability, reducing its degradation by the 26S proteasome, as indicated by MG132 treatments.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Part of the ABA-dependent signaling pathway mediating drought-responsive gene expression via binding to ABRE elements and activating RD29B; positioned downstream of Ca2+-dependent kinase BnaCPK5.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: BnaCPK5 --(protein phosphorylation, activation of transcriptional function and stabilization)--> BnaABF3; BnaABF3 --(transcriptional activation)--> RD29B promoter-driven LUC expression in the ProRD29B::LUC reporter system.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ABA-responsive element binding factor (ABF/AREB), a basic leucine zipper (bZIP) family transcription factor that binds ABRE motifs in promoters of ABA- and drought-inducible genes.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nuclear localization inferred from BiFC assays where YFPn-BnaCPK5 and BnaABF3-YFPc reconstituted YFP signals that overlapped with the nuclear marker NLS-mCherry in N. benthamiana epidermal cells.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BnaCPK5, which interacts with BnaABF3 in nuclei as shown by BiFC and Co-IP, and phosphorylates BnaABF3 in vitro.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Proteasome inhibitor MG132 (50 μM) used to block 26S proteasome-dependent degradation in protein stability assays; luciferin applied to visualize LUC activity; transient expression performed under standard growth conditions in N. benthamiana leaves.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: ABA signaling and transcriptional regulation of drought-responsive gene expression via ABRE elements in Brassica napus.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress response (drought-responsive ABA signaling and gene expression)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In dual-luciferase assays with ProRD29B::LUC, co-expression of BnaABF3 with BnaCPK5 resulted in significantly higher LUC/REN ratios compared with BnaABF3 alone or GFP control effector (P < 0.05), indicating increased transcriptional activity; in modified dual-luciferase protein stability assays, co-expression with BnaCPK5 significantly increased LUC/REN ratios of BnaABF3-LUC fusion proteins relative to GFP control, and this difference was abolished by MG132 treatment.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: BnaABF3 constructs expressed in N. benthamiana leaves for BiFC, Co-IP, dual-luciferase transcriptional activity assays, and protein stability assays; recombinant GST-BnaABF3 expressed in E. coli BL21(Codon Plus) for in vitro kinase assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: DH12075 (source plant for gene cloning and constructs)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Cloning into BiFC vectors (YFPc fusions), Co-IP constructs with cMyc tags, expression and purification of GST-BnaABF3, fluorometric kinase assays with BnaCPK5, dual-luciferase reporter assays using the AtRD29B promoter driving LUC with BnaABF3 as effector and BnaCPK5 or GFP as upstream kinase, and modified dual-luciferase protein stability assays using BnaABF3-LUC fusions with or without proteasome inhibitor MG132.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Full-length coding sequence of BnaABF3 was amplified and subcloned into expression vectors for BiFC, Co-IP, in vitro kinase assays, and luciferase-based reporter and protein stability assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a core ABA-signaling transcription factor whose activity and stability are enhanced by BnaCPK5, BnaABF3 represents a downstream node through which BnaCPK5 can upregulate LEA-like RD29B and other stress-responsive genes; coordinated manipulation of BnaABF3 and BnaCPK5 could be exploited to fine-tune ABA-responsive transcriptional networks and improve drought tolerance in oilseed rape.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The enhanced transcriptional activity and stability of BnaABF4 upon phosphorylation by BnaCPK5 parallel the regulation observed for BnaABF3, suggesting that BnaCPK5 coordinates a subset of ABF/AREB transcription factors to fine-tune ABA and drought responses through both activation and controlled turnover.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. BnaABF4 is an ABA-responsive bZIP transcription factor (ABF/AREB) from oilseed rape that interacts strongly with BnaCPK5 in the nucleus as demonstrated by BiFC and Co-IP.
2. BnaCPK5 directly phosphorylates BnaABF4 in vitro, as shown by kinase assays using recombinant proteins.
3. Co-expression of BnaABF4 with BnaCPK5 in a ProRD29B::LUC dual-luciferase assay leads to significantly increased RD29B-driven luciferase activity compared with BnaABF4 alone or GFP control, indicating that phosphorylation by BnaCPK5 enhances BnaABF4 transcriptional activity.
4. In protein stability assays, co-expression with BnaCPK5 increases the accumulation and LUC activity of BnaABF4-LUC fusion proteins, and this stabilization is lost when the proteasome inhibitor MG132 is applied, indicating that BnaABF4 is subject to 26S proteasome-dependent degradation and that phosphorylation by BnaCPK5 promotes its stability.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BnaABF4 is a nuclear ABA-responsive transcription factor whose phosphorylation by BnaCPK5 increases its transcriptional activity toward the RD29B promoter and enhances its protein stability, supporting a role in promoting ABA-dependent drought-responsive gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: As an ABF/AREB bZIP transcription factor, BnaABF4 binds ABRE motifs to activate RD29B transcription; BnaCPK5-mediated phosphorylation enhances BnaABF4 transcriptional activity and stabilizes the protein, counteracting 26S proteasome-dependent degradation revealed by MG132 treatment.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Component of the ABA-dependent signaling pathway controlling drought-responsive gene expression via ABREs, acting downstream of the Ca2+-dependent kinase BnaCPK5.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: BnaCPK5 --(protein phosphorylation, activation of transcriptional function and stabilization)--> BnaABF4; BnaABF4 --(transcriptional activation)--> RD29B promoter-driven LUC expression in the ProRD29B::LUC reporter system.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ABA-responsive element binding factor (ABF/AREB), basic leucine zipper (bZIP) transcription factor binding ABRE elements in ABA- and drought-inducible promoters.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nuclear localization indicated by BiFC, in which YFPn-BnaCPK5 and BnaABF4-YFPc reconstituted YFP fluorescence co-localizing with NLS-mCherry in nuclei of N. benthamiana epidermal cells.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BnaCPK5, which forms nuclear complexes with BnaABF4 as shown by BiFC and Co-IP, and phosphorylates BnaABF4 in vitro.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Proteasome inhibitor MG132 (50 μM) applied to test 26S proteasome-dependent degradation of BnaABF4-LUC; luciferin application to leaves to visualize LUC activity during transient expression assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: ABA signaling and transcriptional regulation of drought-responsive genes via ABRE elements in Brassica napus.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress response (drought-responsive ABA signaling and gene expression)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In dual-luciferase assays, co-expression of BnaABF4 with BnaCPK5 significantly increased RD29B-driven LUC/REN ratios compared with BnaABF4 alone or GFP control effector (P < 0.05); in protein stability assays, BnaCPK5 co-expression significantly elevated LUC/REN ratios of BnaABF4-LUC fusion proteins relative to GFP control, with the difference abolished by MG132 treatment.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: BnaABF4 constructs expressed in N. benthamiana leaves for BiFC, Co-IP, dual-luciferase transcriptional activity assays, and protein stability assays; recombinant GST-BnaABF4 expressed in E. coli BL21(Codon Plus) for in vitro kinase assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: DH12075 (source plant for gene cloning and constructs)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Generation of YFPc-, cMyc-, GST-, and LUC-fusion constructs of BnaABF4; BiFC assays with YFPn-BnaCPK5; Co-IP with BnaCPK5-HA; fluorometric kinase assays using GST-BnaABF4 as substrate of BnaCPK5; dual-luciferase assays with ProRD29B::LUC reporter and BnaABF4 with or without BnaCPK5; modified dual-luciferase protein stability assays using BnaABF4-LUC fusions and MG132 treatments.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Full-length coding sequence of BnaABF4 was amplified and subcloned into BiFC, Co-IP, GST-fusion, and luciferase reporter vectors for interaction, phosphorylation, transcriptional activity, and protein stability assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: BnaABF4, together with BnaABF3, constitutes a core ABA-signaling transcription factor targeted by BnaCPK5; manipulating BnaABF4 activity or its phosphorylation status offers a means to modulate ABA-responsive gene expression (including LEA-like RD29B) and may contribute to engineering improved drought tolerance in oilseed rape when combined with modulation of BnaCPK5.
